title: descurainia richardsonii subsp. incisa
intro: Descurainia richardsonii subsp. incisa, also known as Richardson's wallflower or cut-leaved whitlowgrass, is a plant species that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Descurainia richardsonii subsp. incisa include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and salves.
